name: Wire VPC into environments

goal.answer: Call the reusable VPC module from the dev and prod Terraform root modules, populate environment-specific terraform.tfvars with CIDRs and AZs from docs/technical-spec.md, and verify the configuration with terraform fmt and terraform validate.

criteria.answer:
terraform/environments/dev/main.tf calls module "vpc" with source "../../modules/vpc" and passes environment-specific inputs
terraform/environments/prod/main.tf calls module "vpc" with source "../../modules/vpc" and passes environment-specific inputs
terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars defines vpc_cidr, public_subnet_cidrs, and availability_zones matching docs/technical-spec.md
terraform/environments/prod/terraform.tfvars defines vpc_cidr, public_subnet_cidrs, and availability_zones matching docs/technical-spec.md
terraform fmt -recursive -check terraform/ passes
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/prod

constraints.answer: Use existing project conventions: provider ~> 5.0, required_version >= 1.6.0, naming petclinic-{env}-{resource}, required tags Project/Environment/ManagedBy, CIDRs must match docs/technical-spec.md exactly: dev 10.0.0.0/16 (subnets 10.0.1.0/24, 10.0.2.0/24), prod 10.1.0.0/16 (subnets 10.1.1.0/24, 10.1.2.0/24), Availability zones must be us-east-2a and us-east-2b per docs/technical-spec.md, Do not add NAT Gateways, private subnets, or VPC endpoints, Do not modify the application repo

assumptions.answer: The reusable VPC module in terraform/modules/vpc/ is complete and functional; Provider and backend configuration in terraform/environments/dev/ and prod/ already exists from Epic E-1; AWS region us-east-2 is used for both environments; terraform init has already been run in both environment directories

phases.name: Wire VPC into environments
phases.goal: Integrate the VPC module into dev and prod root modules and validate the configuration

steps.description: Update terraform/environments/dev/main.tf to call the vpc module and pass dev-specific inputs
Create terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars with dev VPC CIDR, public subnets, and AZs
Update terraform/environments/prod/main.tf to call the vpc module and pass prod-specific inputs
Create terraform/environments/prod/terraform.tfvars with prod VPC CIDR, public subnets, and AZs

phases.summary: Phase 1 complete: VPC module wired into dev and prod root modules, environment-specific terraform.tfvars populated with CIDRs and AZs from docs/technical-spec.md, and both environments pass terraform fmt and terraform validate.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

goal: Call the reusable VPC module from the dev and prod Terraform root modules, populate environment-specific terraform.tfvars with CIDRs and AZs from docs/technical-spec.md, and verify the configuration with terraform fmt and terraform validate.
successCriteria: terraform/environments/dev/main.tf calls module "vpc" with source "../../modules/vpc" and passes environment-specific inputs
terraform/environments/prod/main.tf calls module "vpc" with source "../../modules/vpc" and passes environment-specific inputs
terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars defines vpc_cidr, public_subnet_cidrs, and availability_zones matching docs/technical-spec.md
terraform/environments/prod/terraform.tfvars defines vpc_cidr, public_subnet_cidrs, and availability_zones matching docs/technical-spec.md
constraints: Use existing project conventions: provider ~> 5.0, required_version >= 1.6.0, naming petclinic-{env}-{resource}, required tags Project/Environment/ManagedBy
CIDRs must match docs/technical-spec.md exactly: dev 10.0.0.0/16 (subnets 10.0.1.0/24, 10.0.2.0/24), prod 10.1.0.0/16 (subnets 10.1.1.0/24, 10.1.2.0/24)
Availability zones must be us-east-2a and us-east-2b per docs/technical-spec.md
Do not add NAT Gateways, private subnets, or VPC endpoints
Do not modify the application repo

grade.rationale: Although phase gates F1–F3 and C1–C3 assert real terraform fmt/validate runs and correct module wiring, the supplied diff is dominated by unrelated region-string churn (e.g., .claude/rules/helm.md, .claude/rules/kubernetes.md, docs/technical-spec.md) and truncates the Terraform changes. The required terraform/environments/{dev,prod}/terraform.tfvars and reusable VPC module contents are not visible in the evidence, so the success criteria are asserted rather than substantiated.
